ogTitle: Macdonald Park vs Magarey: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Macdonald Park and Magarey.
heading: Macdonald Park vs Magarey — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Macdonald Park, South Australia and Magarey, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Macdonald Park has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Magarey (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Macdonald Park or Magarey safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Macdonald Park has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Magarey scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Macdonald Park scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Magarey scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Macdonald Park or Magarey?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Macdonald Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
